complaint_templates:
Subject: Slow response times
From: user{id}@cascade.com

Hi, our team is experiencing significant delays accessing the {service}. This has been going on for the past hour. Can someone look into this?

Subject: Connection timeout
From: client{id}@external.com

We're getting connection timeouts on our end. Our SLA is being violated. Please escalate urgently.

Subject: File access lag
From: user{id}@cascade.com

Uploading files to the shared drive takes 3x longer than usual. Is there a problem with the storage servers?

Subject: Transaction delays
From: partner{id}@partner.com

Our API calls to your endpoints are timing out. This is affecting our production pipeline.

Subject: Service degradation
From: admin{id}@cascade.com

Monitoring shows increased latency on segment {segment}. Users are complaining. Please investigate ASAP.

stage_0_complete.subject: Training complete — Stand by
stage_0_complete.body:
Good work, operator. You've passed the initial assessment. Stand by for assignment.

— Training Assistant v2.1

stage_1_start.subject: Shift handoff — Night duty
stage_1_start.body:
Operator,

Your shift begins at 22:00. Reports of slow data access on DMZ segment. Please investigate.

Standard protocols apply.

— Cascade Ops Scheduler

stage_2_escalation.subject: Re: Performance issues — multiple nodes
stage_2_escalation.body:
I'm seeing complaints from multiple clients now. What's going on? Get this under control.

— T. Chen, NetSec Lead

stage_3_mirror.subject: Network anomalies detected
stage_3_mirror.body:
Routing team noticed asymmetric paths in the DMZ segment. Are you messing with the route tables? Explain.

— T. Chen, NetSec Lead

stage_4_crontab.subject: ALERT: Unauthorized cron entries
stage_4_crontab.body:
Security monitoring has detected unauthorized cron modifications on multiple nodes. Possible persistence mechanism. Investigate and revert.

— Security Monitor (Automated)

stage_5_archive.subject: Archive segment access logged
stage_5_archive.body:
Unauthorized access attempt logged on archive segment. Origin: internal network. Cross-reference with active sessions.

— Audit System (Automated)

stage_6_decision.subject: You found it.
stage_6_decision.body:
You've seen the Echo project. Now you know what Cascade is doing.

The files in /archive/echo/ contain the proof. Help me expose them, or bury it forever.

The choice is yours.

— Mirage

ending_beat_virus.subject: Excellent work
ending_beat_virus.body:
Operator,

I've reviewed the incident report. You identified the threat, neutralized the watchdog, and stabilized the network routes. The outbreak has been contained.

Cascade's security team will handle the remaining cleanup. Take the rest of the night off.

— T. Chen, NetSec Lead

ending_virus_wins.subject: It's over
ending_virus_wins.body:
Operator,

The network has been fully compromised. We're shutting down all systems effective immediately.

You are relieved of duty. This is not your fault — but someone will have to answer for it.

— D. Prescott, CEO

usat_warning_40.subject: WARNING: User satisfaction dropping
usat_warning_40.body:
Operator, user satisfaction is at {usat}%. This is below acceptable thresholds. I expect this to improve immediately.

— T. Chen, NetSec Lead

usat_critical_25.subject: CRITICAL: Emergency network restoration
usat_critical_25.body:
Attention! User satisfaction has dropped to critical levels. All isolated servers have been returned to the network to ensure business continuity.

Your access to isolation controls is temporarily restricted.

— Cascade Operations Management

usat_firing_15.subject: Notice of Termination Proceedings
usat_firing_15.body:
Operator,

Due to gross negligence resulting in severe service degradation, termination proceedings have been initiated. You have 5 minutes to vacate the premises.

Your access credentials will be revoked at the end of this shift.

— Human Resources, Cascade Dynamics

ending_expose.subject: The truth is out
ending_expose.body:
The data has been published. Cascade's Echo project is exposed.

Thank you, operator. You did the right thing.

— Alexei Werner (Mirage)

ending_loyalty.subject: Commendation
ending_loyalty.body:
Operator,

Your handling of the situation did not go unnoticed. The threat has been neutralized, and your loyalty to Cascade is appreciated.

A bonus has been added to your next paycheck.

— D. Prescott, CEO

ending_shadow.body:
I see what you did. The data exists in two places now.

Neither side knows about the third copy.

Interesting choice.

— ???

ending_firefighting.subject: Termination Confirmation
ending_firefighting.body:
Operator,

Your employment with Cascade Dynamics is terminated effective immediately. Security will escort you out.

The virus was stopped. The company was not saved.

— Human Resources, Cascade Dynamics

stage_0.content:
Welcome to the night shift, operator. I'm your training assistant. Let's run through the basics.

First, try connecting to a server: `ssh admin@10.0.1.1`

stage_1.content: [SYSTEM] New shift started. Monitoring shows normal baseline on all segments.

I'm reading reports of slow data access on DMZ segment. Check the processes on dmz-01.

Try: `ssh admin@10.0.1.1` then `top` or `ps aux` to see what's running.

stage_1_first_infection.content: [ALERT] Unknown process 'bloomd' detected on dmz-03 (10.0.1.3) — CPU load 12%.

That's not a standard process. Investigate it. Use `ps aux | grep bloomd` to find the PID, then `kill -9 <PID>`.

Also search for suspicious files with `find / -name "*.bloomd*" -type f 2>/dev/null`

stage_2.content: [ALERT] Bloomd detected on multiple DMZ nodes. Possible propagation in progress.

The virus is spreading. You can isolate nodes to contain it: connect to the segment router and use `ifconfig eth0 down`.

But be careful — each isolation reduces user satisfaction (USAT). Check USAT in the status bar.

stage_3.content: [ALERT] Watchdog process detected. Virus recovery mechanism active.

A watchdog is restarting the virus. Use `ps aux | grep watchdog` to find it, then kill it BEFORE removing the virus file.

Also — network routes are behaving oddly. Try `traceroute` to trace the path.

stage_4.content: [ALERT] Crontab modifications detected on infected nodes. Virus achieving persistence.

The virus is writing to crontab. Check `/etc/crontab` on infected nodes and remove the malicious entries.

Also look for entries in `/etc/rc.local`.

stage_5.content: [ALERT] Unknown directory accessed on archive segment. Cross-referencing access logs.
[DISCOVERY] Hidden project directory found: /archive/echo/ on core-14

stage_6.content: [SYSTEM] Echo project files located at /archive/echo/ on core-14. Contents: project_echo.pdf, journal.txt, client_data/
